verses.text: Threatened by Baasha, Asa — who once trusted God against a million — now strips the temple treasury to hire Ben-hadad of Syria. He leans on a pagan king instead of the LORD. Past faith is no guarantee of present faith; the heart must trust God afresh in each new trial.

Threatened by Baasha, Asa — who once trusted God against a million — now strips the temple treasury to hire Ben-hadad of Syria. He leans on a pagan king instead of the LORD. Past faith is no guarantee of present faith; the heart must trust God afresh in each new trial.

Threatened by Baasha, Asa — who once trusted God against a million — now strips the temple treasury to hire Ben-hadad of Syria. He leans on a pagan king instead of the LORD. Past faith is no guarantee of present faith; the heart must trust God afresh in each new trial.

The scheme works: Ben-hadad strikes Israel, Baasha halts, and Asa carries off the stones of Ramah. The worldly alliance brings quick relief. Yet success by unbelieving means is the most dangerous kind, for it tempts us to call our sin wisdom.

The scheme works: Ben-hadad strikes Israel, Baasha halts, and Asa carries off the stones of Ramah. The worldly alliance brings quick relief. Yet success by unbelieving means is the most dangerous kind, for it tempts us to call our sin wisdom.

The scheme works: Ben-hadad strikes Israel, Baasha halts, and Asa carries off the stones of Ramah. The worldly alliance brings quick relief. Yet success by unbelieving means is the most dangerous kind, for it tempts us to call our sin wisdom.

Hanani the seer reproves Asa: because he relied on Syria and not the LORD, whose 'eyes run to and fro throughout the whole earth' to strengthen the upright, he has done foolishly and shall have wars. God seeks hearts wholly His. To trade His help for man's is a poor bargain.

Hanani the seer reproves Asa: because he relied on Syria and not the LORD, whose 'eyes run to and fro throughout the whole earth' to strengthen the upright, he has done foolishly and shall have wars. God seeks hearts wholly His. To trade His help for man's is a poor bargain.

Hanani the seer reproves Asa: because he relied on Syria and not the LORD, whose 'eyes run to and fro throughout the whole earth' to strengthen the upright, he has done foolishly and shall have wars. God seeks hearts wholly His. To trade His help for man's is a poor bargain.

Stung by the rebuke, Asa imprisons the seer and oppresses some of the people. Pride will not bear correction. The same heart that turns from God grows hard against His messengers, adding cruelty to unbelief.

In old age Asa is diseased in his feet, 'yet in his disease he sought not to the LORD, but to the physicians,' and dies. The man of early faith ends leaning on everything but God. How we finish matters; perseverance, not merely a good beginning, proves the heart.
